instruction:
Given that
$$$\sum^{491}_{a=1}\sum^{491}_{b=1}\frac{1}{(a + bi)^4 - 1}$$$
can be expressed in the form $\frac{p+qi}{r}$ , where gcd $(p, q, r) = 1$ and $r > 0$ , find the unique integer $k$ between $0$ and $982$ inclusive such that $983$ divides $(p+q)-kr$ . [Note: $983$ is a prime.]

Options:
A. 858
B. 855
C. 854
D. 853
E. 857
F. 860
G. 856
H. 851
I. 852
J. 859
